name: Netherlands
full: Netherlands — TenneT & RVO Flexibility & Grid Innovation
sub: Netherlands leads Europe in grid flexibility procurement and demand-side response. TenneT's 12,000+ queue drives congestion solutions. RVO funds €350M+ in innovation. Alliander scales AI to 2,500+ smart contracts in 2025.
desc: TenneT operates both Dutch and German grids. Its congestion management service (take-and-feed) actively engages technology partners. RVO (Netherlands Enterprise Agency) runs €350M+ innovation portfolio across MOOI, Flex-e, and DEI+. Alliander, largest Dutch DSO, deployed gridX AI across 100+ local grids and is scaling XENON platform to 2,500 smart contracts in 2025. No local entity required for TenneT or Alliander partnerships.

studies.label: TenneT NL Congestion Management
studies.text:
What it doesTenneT operates 12,000+ MW interconnection queue in the Netherlands. Launched 'take-and-feed' service (2024) that actively manages grid congestion through real-time flexibility dispatch. Commercial partnerships with tech providers — no nationality restriction.Why it mattersDirect route to procure capacity reduction solutions. TenneT has explicit AI integration roadmap.
TenneT Take-and-Feed
studies.deadline: Rolling — commercial congestion management procurement ongoing. Take-and-feed service operational since 2024.
studies.budgetBasis: TenneT NL does not publish per-contract values. Estimated from congestion management service procurement scales and comparable European TSO technology contracts.

eligibility.text: Commercial partnerships with tech providers — no nationality restriction. TenneT has explicit AI integration roadmap.

relevance.text: TenneT operates 12,000+ MW interconnection queue. Take-and-feed service directly procures congestion management solutions — exact fit for AI queue management.

applicationRoute.detail: Direct partnership approach for congestion solutions
Frame around take-and-feed service and queue reduction

studies.label: RVO MOOI (Modernisation of Electricity Supply)
studies.text:
What it doesPart of RVO's €350M innovation portfolio. MOOI specifically targets electricity grid modernisation including congestion management, predictive analytics, and AI integration. Grants up to €3M per project. €90M+ annually allocated.How to applyRequires Dutch partner entity. Annual calls with 6-8 week decision cycle.
RVO MOOI Portal
studies.deadline: 2026 call: Preliminary applications 17 March - 16 April 2026. Full applications 2 June - 3 September 2026. Consortia of 3+ companies required.

eligibility.text: Requires Dutch partner entity in consortium. Annual calls with 6-8 week decision cycle.

relevance.text: Specifically targets electricity grid modernisation including congestion management, predictive analytics, and AI integration.

applicationRoute.detail: Up to €4M for grid modernisation research

studies.label: LV-NExT (Low Voltage Network Exploration & Experimentation Toolkit)
studies.text:
What it doesConsortium programme (2023-2027) deploying smart metering & AI forecasting across Dutch low-voltage networks. Open to technology partnerships. Focus: demand forecasting, congestion prediction, and automated response.
LV-NExT Platform
studies.deadline: Ongoing consortium programme (2023-2027). Technology partnerships accepted on rolling basis.
studies.budgetBasis: Consortium does not publish per-partner budgets. Estimated from 4-year programme scope and typical Dutch DSO innovation partnership scales.

eligibility.text: Open to technology partnerships. No entity requirement for partnership participation.

relevance.text: Deploying smart metering and AI forecasting across Dutch low-voltage networks. Demand forecasting and congestion prediction directly relevant.

applicationRoute.detail: Aligned to demand forecasting or congestion prediction
Evaluation of solution fit for LV network challenges
Access to DSO test beds and smart metering data

studies.label: RVO Flex-e (Flexibility Innovation)
studies.text:
What it does€70M+ dedicated to flexibility and demand response innovation. Smaller projects (€100K-€500K) with rolling applications. Direct DSO partnerships. Explicit support for AI load forecasting and automated curtailment.
Flex-e Fund
studies.deadline: Rolling applications — submit via rvo.nl. Fast-track assessment with 6-8 week decision cycle.

eligibility.text: Requires Dutch partner or entity. Rolling applications with fast decision cycle.

relevance.text: Dedicated to flexibility and demand response innovation. Explicit support for AI load forecasting and automated curtailment.

studies.label: RVO DEI+ (Energy & Climate Innovation)
studies.text:
What it doesBroader energy innovation fund (€80M+). Supports grid digitalisation, system integration, and cross-sector energy projects. Consortia-based, larger scale.
DEI+ Portal
studies.deadline: Periodic calls via rvo.nl. Check topsectorenergie.nl for 2026 energy innovation subsidy calendar.

eligibility.text: Consortia-based — requires Dutch lead partner. Larger scale than Flex-e.

relevance.text: Broader energy innovation fund covering grid digitalisation and system integration. Less targeted than MOOI or Flex-e for specific grid interconnection.

applicationRoute.step: Form consortium with Dutch lead partner

applicationRoute.detail: Larger scale, longer duration than Flex-e

faq.q: Can we access RVO funding without a Dutch entity?
faq.a: You need at least one Dutch partner in the consortium. RVO-funded projects must be led by or include a Dutch organisation. Alliander and TenneT partnerships don't require this.

faq.q: How active is TenneT in technology partnerships?
faq.a: Very active. They operate 12,000+ MW queue and explicitly use commercial technology partners for congestion management. The take-and-feed service (2024) is their primary commercial engagement vehicle.

faq.q: What's the difference between Flex-e and MOOI?
faq.a: Flex-e targets flexibility/demand-response (€70M), with smaller projects and faster decisions. MOOI focuses on grid modernisation (€90M), with larger multiyear projects. Both are RVO-administered.

faq.q: How many smart contracts is Alliander aiming to deploy?
faq.a: Alliander is scaling XENON (gridX AI) from 100+ contracts in 2024 to 2,500+ contracts in 2025. This represents exponential expansion of the addressable market.

note: Netherlands is Europe's leading flexibility market. TenneT and Alliander partnerships don't require local entity. RVO funds (Flex-e, MOOI, DEI+) offer €240M+ but require Dutch partner.
